batch_summary: Deed [317] — Warranty deed, Clara Barton Miller (joined by husband Roy D. Miller) to Virgil L Bounds, 60.4 ac William Ritchie League A-527, Freestone County, February 22, 1973. Conveys surface estate plus 15-acre non-participating royalty interest; grantor reserves ALL other minerals including exclusive leasing rights forever. Metes-and-bounds analysis establishes this is Lot 2 of the McClelland partition (Partition Judgment No. 8153-B, 1926), NOT Lot 3 as previously assumed for the 60.4-ac parent tract. This distinction is critical: if the Beldin Tract (F15410, 33.66 ac) originates from Lot 2 rather than Lot 3, the lot attribution in the property inventory needs correction, though Miller's mineral reservation does not cloud the Beldin minerals because the 33.66-ac carve-out predates Miller's chain by decades. Source deed identified: J.P. Stubbs + Ethel Stubbs to Mrs. J.P. Jackson et al. (1937, Vol. 320 Pg. 27 FC). FC index citation corrected from DR/422/225 to DR/422/491. ISSUE-43 item 26 satisfied; ISSUE-44 advanced with Lot 2/Lot 3 finding — partition judgment pull now critical priority.

02_PROPERTY_INVENTORY.md.timestamp_summary: Deed [317] — Lot 2 vs Lot 3 distinction identified in McClelland partition of 181.3-ac Richie League tract; Miller mineral reservation noted; source deed chain (Stubbs→Jackson 1937) documented

operations.description: Add note about Lot 2/Lot 3 distinction from deed [317] metes-and-bounds analysis to Richie League group section
operations.content: **⚠️ Lot 2 / Lot 3 partition distinction — deed [317]** (2026-03-31): Deed [317] (1973-02-22, Clara Barton Miller → Virgil L Bounds, DR/422/491 FC) conveys 60.4 ac of the Wm. Richie A-527 — but metes-and-bounds analysis shows it is **Lot 2** of the McClelland partition, NOT Lot 3. The deed begins at the SW corner of the 181.3-ac tract (vs. deed [313] which begins at the SE corner for Lot 3). Corner calls reference common corners of Lots 1, 2, and 3, confirming this is the middle lot. Both Lots 2 and 3 are 60.4 ac. The F15410 chain notes currently attribute the Beldin Tract to "Lot No. 3" — this needs verification against the partition judgment. If the McLelland→Beldin chain (1926) originates from Lot 2, the lot number attribution should be corrected. **Miller mineral reservation**: Clara Barton Miller reserved ALL minerals except a 15-ac NPRI, including exclusive leasing rights forever. This reservation does NOT cloud the Beldin Tract if the 33.66-ac Beldin carve-out predates Miller's chain (McLelland→Beldin 1926 vs. Stubbs→Jackson 1937 → Miller → Virgil 1973). Source deed for Lot 2: J.P. Stubbs + Ethel Stubbs → Mrs. J.P. Jackson et al., Jan 18, 1937, Vol. 320 Pg. 27 FC. Highway exception: part conveyed to State of Texas, Vol. 192 Pg. 199 FC. **Action**: Pull T.A. Bounds estate partition judgment (Case No. 8153-B, 1/28/1926) to confirm lot assignments — ISSUE-44.

operations.description: Add [317] to Phase 1 deed list in Phase Structure Overview table
Add deed [317] entry to Phase 1 section, after deed [313] and before Phase 2 header
operations.content: 19. `1973-02-22_[317]_f` — Clara Barton Miller (joined by husband Roy D. Miller) → Virgil L Bounds (warranty deed, **surface + 15-ac NPRI only**, Freestone) — 60.4 acres, William Ritchie League, A-527, Freestone County. **Lot 2** of the McClelland partition of 181.3-acre T.A. Bounds estate tract (Partition Judgment No. 8153-B, Rufus McClelland et al. vs. J.V. Bounds Jr. et al., January 28, 1926, Vol. 1 Pg. 235, 87th Judicial District Court FC). Metes-and-bounds: beginning at SW corner of original 181.3-acre tract on public road; N 34-30 E with public road 330 vrs to common corner of Lots 2 and 3 in partition by McClelland heirs; N 56-20 W 1026.3 vrs to E line of Lot 1, common corner of Lots 2 and 3; S 34-20 W 334 vrs to common corner of Lots 1 and 2; S 56-36 E 1027.4 vrs to POB. Containing 60.4 acres more or less. **Interest conveyed**: Surface estate + 15-acre non-participating royalty interest. **MINERAL RESERVATION**: "all other minerals under said land is hereby reserved unto Grantor, Clara Barton Miller, her heirs and assigns including the exclusive right to lease said land and receive the bonus and rentals under said leases forever." Source deed: J. P. Stubbs and wife Ethel Stubbs → Mrs. J. P. Jackson et al., January 18, 1937, Vol. 320, Pg. 27, Deed Records FC. Exception: part conveyed to State of Texas per Vol. 192, Pg. 199, Deed Records FC. Consideration: $10.00 + OGVC. Both grantors of Freestone County. ⚠️ **LOT 2 / LOT 3 DISTINCTION**: This deed begins at the SW corner of the 181.3-ac tract; deed [313] (Lot 3, also 60.4 ac) begins at the SE corner. Lots 2 and 3 are adjacent, sharing their NW-SE boundary (1026.3 vrs). The Beldin Tract (F15410, 33.66 ac) is currently attributed to Lot 3 but the McLelland→Beldin chain (1926) may originate from Lot 2 — HUMAN VERIFICATION REQUIRED via partition judgment pull. ⚠️ **FC index citation discrepancy**: ISSUE-43 item 26 listed this as DR/422/225; actual deed pagination is Deed Record 422 Pg. 491–492 (Doc# 478). Acknowledged Feb 22, 1973 before Ernest M Jones NP, Freestone County. Filed Feb 22, 1973 at 1:00 PM; recorded Feb 23, 1973 at 10:10 AM; Henry McCormick, County Clerk, Freestone County; Deputy: Sue Lambert. ISSUE-43 item 26 satisfied. ISSUE-44 advanced (Lot 2 identification + source deed chain + mineral severance). Phase 1

04_MINERAL_RIGHTS_TRACKER.md.timestamp_summary: Deed [317] — Miller mineral reservation on Lot 2 (60.4 ac Richie League) documented; impact on Beldin Tract assessed (no cloud if Beldin carved pre-Miller chain)

operations.description: Add Miller mineral reservation note to Beldin Tract mineral section, before Edwards Tract section
operations.anchor: ### Edwards Tract (85.30 acres, Freestone, F17558) [Sarah McAnulty Survey]
operations.content:
- **⚠️ Clara Barton Miller mineral reservation — deed [317]** (2026-03-31): Deed [317] (DR/422/491 FC, 1973-02-22) conveys 60.4 ac Wm. Richie A-527 from Clara Barton Miller to Virgil L Bounds — **surface + 15-ac NPRI only**. Miller reserved ALL other minerals including exclusive leasing rights forever. Metes-and-bounds analysis establishes this is **Lot 2** of the McClelland partition, NOT Lot 3. If the Beldin Tract (33.66 ac, F15410) originates from Lot 2, Miller's reservation does NOT cloud the Beldin minerals because the McLelland→Beldin carve-out (1926) predates Miller's source chain (Stubbs→Jackson 1937). Miller's 60.4-ac description is likely an over-description of the lot after the Beldin separation. However, **this depends on the Beldin Tract's lot of origin** — pull partition judgment (Case No. 8153-B) to confirm. If Beldin is from Lot 3 (as currently attributed), Miller's reservation has no connection to any current holdings. See ISSUE-44.

---

operations.replace_with:
- **Key finding — deed [317] (1973 Miller → Virgil warranty deed)**: Deed [317] (DR/422/491 FC, 1973-02-22, Clara Barton Miller → Virgil L Bounds) conveys 60.4 ac Wm. Richie A-527. Metes-and-bounds analysis establishes this is **Lot 2** of the McClelland partition, NOT Lot 3. Deed [317] begins at the SW corner of the 181.3-ac tract; deed [313] (Lot 3) begins at the SE corner. Corner calls explicitly reference "common corner for Lots two and three" and "common corner of Lots 1 and 2," tracing the boundaries of the middle lot. Both lots are 60.4 ac. **Critical implication**: If the Beldin Tract (33.66 ac) originates from Lot 2 (McLelland allotment), the "Lot No. 3" attribution in F15410 chain notes and in this issue's description needs correction. **Miller mineral reservation**: Miller reserved ALL minerals except a 15-ac NPRI, including exclusive leasing rights forever. This reservation does NOT reach the Beldin Tract if the 33.66-ac Beldin carve-out (McLelland→Beldin 1926) predates Miller's source chain (Stubbs→Jackson 1937). Miller's deed describes 60.4 ac with no exception for the Beldin portion — likely an over-description of the original lot after the 33.66-ac separation. **Upstream chain identified**: J.P. Stubbs + Ethel Stubbs → Mrs. J.P. Jackson et al. (Jan 18, 1937, Vol. 320, Pg. 27, Deed Records FC) → [unknown intermediate conveyances] → Clara Barton Miller → Virgil L Bounds (1973). Highway exception: part of Lot 2 previously conveyed to State of Texas (Vol. 192, Pg. 199 FC). ⚠️ FC index citation stated DR/422/225 — actual deed pagination is DR/422/491 (Doc# 478); index was incorrect. **Partition judgment pull is now CRITICAL PRIORITY** — determines which heir received which lot and resolves the Lot 2/Lot 3 question for the Beldin Tract chain.
- **Pull items**: ISSUE-43 items 25 (1978-2653, J.V. Jr. estate → Virgil L.), ~~26 (1973-478, Miller Clara Barton → Virgil L.)~~ **PULLED ✓ = deed [317]**, 27 (J.V. Jr. will from FC probate). ~~28 (1962-690, Elgin mineral lease)~~ **PULLED ✓ = deed [313]**. NEW: T.A. Bounds estate partition judgment (FC 87th District Court, Case No. 8153-B, Vol. 1 Pg. 235, Jan 28, 1926) — pull from district court minutes to identify all allotments. **PRIORITY ELEVATED — CRITICAL** (determines lot assignments, resolves Lot 2/Lot 3 question).
- **Added**: Trust No. 2 / JAB Scope Expansion Session (2026-03-10)
